title: 11 Best Herbal Teas For Migraine
intro: Herbal teas for Migraine are a natural remedy that involves the consumption of plant-based teas to alleviate the symptoms of migraine headaches. These teas are effective in treating migraines because they contain properties that help to relax tense muscles, reduce inflammation, and improve blood flow, thereby providing relief from the pain and discomfort associated with migraines. Examples of herbal teas that can be used to treat migraines include feverfew, which contains a compound called parthenolide that helps to reduce inflammation and prevent migraine attacks; ginger, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to alleviate pain and nausea; peppermint, which can help to relax tense muscles and improve blood flow; lavender, which has a calming effect that can help to reduce stress and anxiety; and passionflower, which contains a compound called flavonoids that can help to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation. Additionally, other herbal teas such as willow bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, and skullcap, which has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, can also be used to treat migraines.

preparations.herb_desc: Foeniculum vulgare teas, also known as fennel tea, have been used for centuries to treat migraine ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The tea helps to treat migraines by reducing inflammation and relaxing tense muscles, which in turn relieves pain and discomfort. The bioactive constituents of fennel tea, including anethole, limonene, and flavonoids, contribute to its therapeutic effects by modulating neurotransmitters and reducing oxidative stress. The benefits of fennel tea in treating migraines include its ability to provide rapid relief from symptoms, reduce the frequency and severity of attacks, and promote overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 teaspoon of dried Foeniculum vulgare (also known as fennel seeds).
Add the fennel seeds to a teapot or a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the fennel seeds in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and add honey to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea slowly and comfortably to help alleviate migraine symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Foeniculum vulgare teas can be used to alleviate migraine symptoms, but they may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. When using Foeniculum vulgare teas to treat migraines, it is essential to be cautious, as excessive consumption may lead to interactions with other medications, exacerbate digestive issues, and worsen conditions such as diabetes or low blood pressure.
preparation_usage: Consult a healthcare professional before using Foeniculum vulgare tea for migraines.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried Foeniculum vulgare in a cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and let cool to a comfortable temperature.
Drink 2-3 cups of Foeniculum vulgare tea per day, ideally 1 cup in the morning and 1-2 cups throughout the day.
Repeat as needed, ideally up to 3 times a week, under the guidance of a healthcare professional.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas have been traditionally used to treat migraine due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate migraine symptoms such as pain and discomfort. The bioactive constituents of Zingiber officinale, including gingerols and shogaols, possess potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic activities that contribute to its therapeutic effects. By inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and modulating pain-sensing pathways, Zingiber officinale teas help to reduce migraine severity and frequency. The benefits of using Zingiber officinale teas to treat migraine include reduced frequency and severity of attacks, improved quality of life, and a lower reliance on conventional pain medications.
preparation_procedure: Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Take 1 teaspoon of fresh ginger root and slice it into thin pieces.
Add the sliced ginger to the boiling water and let it steep for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the ginger pieces.
Drink the tea hot or let it cool down to your liking.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", Zingiber officinale teas for migraine may exert both abortive and prophylactic effects without any side-effects.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used as a potential remedy for migraines, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its spicy and warming properties. To use Zingiber officinale teas effectively, it's recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase it to minimize side effects, and also to drink plenty of water to help alleviate stomach discomfort and to avoid taking it before bedtime to prevent sleep disturbances.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Zingiber officinale tea 2-3 times a day
Start with 5-7 grams of dried ginger per cup of boiling water
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea before drinking
Drink the tea as soon as you feel a migraine coming on
Continue drinking for 2-3 days or until symptoms improve

preparations.herb_desc: Valeriana officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat migraine ailments due to their sedative, anti-inflammatory, and pain-relieving properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat migraines by reducing muscle tension, relaxing the mind, and alleviating symptoms such as pain, nausea, and sensitivity to light. The bioactive constituents of Valeriana officinalis, including valerenic acid, isovaleric acid, and valepotriates, contribute to its therapeutic effects by modulating the GABA receptor, reducing inflammation, and blocking pain pathways. The benefits of using Valeriana officinalis teas to treat migraines include reduced frequency and severity of episodes, improved sleep quality, and a sense of calm and relaxation.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Valeriana officinalis roots or 2-3 tablespoons of dried roots.
Use a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup to steep the roots in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into another cup, discarding the roots.
Add honey to taste, if desired, to make the tea sweeter.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate migraine symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Valeriana officinalis teas may be beneficial in reducing pain associated with migraines, as its root extract showed significant analgesic effects in both acute and chronic phases of pain.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Valeriana officinalis teas can be effective in alleviating migraine symptoms, but possible side effects include drowsiness, stomach upset, and interactions with certain medications that may exacerbate the condition. To use Valeriana officinalis teas safely, it is essential to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, avoid consuming it before driving or operating heavy machinery, and be aware of potential interactions with other herbal remedies or medications.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Valeriana officinalis tea for migraine relief.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried root per cup of boiling water, 2-3 times a day.
Drink tea as soon as symptoms appear, or before bedtime to aid sleep.
Gradually increase dosage to 2 teaspoons per cup if needed, but not exceeding 3 times a day.
Monitor migraine frequency and adjust dosage or consult doctor if symptoms persist.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat migraines due to their calming and analgesic properties, which help to soothe the nervous system and alleviate pain. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as linalool and linalyl acetate, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, helping to reduce migraine symptoms. The benefits of using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat migraines include reduced frequency and severity of attacks, improved sleep quality, and a decrease in anxiety and stress levels, leading to a better overall quality of life. By incorporating Lavandula angustifolia teas into one's treatment plan, individuals may experience a reduction in migraine-related discomfort and an improvement in their overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers.
Use 2 tablespoons of the dried flowers for every cup of boiling water.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot and bring it to a boil.
Add the 2 tablespoons of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and let it cool down before drinking.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used as a potential treatment for migraines, but it may cause side effects such as drowsiness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with small doses, avoid consuming the tea before driving or operating heavy machinery, and discontinue use if any adverse reactions occur.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor before using Lavandula angustifolia tea for migraines
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried flowers per cup of boiling water
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink
Drink 1-2 cups of tea, 2-3 times a day, as needed
Continue for 7-10 days, then assess and adjust as needed

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat migraine ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties. This herbal preparation helps to treat migraine by improving blood circulation, reducing blood vessel spasms, and alleviating pain. The bioactive constituents of Ginkgo biloba, including flavonoids, terpenoids, and bilobalide, contribute to its therapeutic effects by inhibiting platelet aggregation and enhancing nitric oxide production, ultimately leading to vasodilation and pain relief. Regular consumption of Ginkgo biloba teas has been associated with reduced frequency and severity of migraines, making it a popular natural remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Boil the water in a kettle or on the stove.
Add the Ginkgo biloba leaves to a tea infuser or a small muslin bag.
Steep the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1 cup, 2-3 times a day, to help with migraines.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Presse medicale (Paris, France : 1983)", Ginkgo biloba teas for migraine may provide significant pain relief and improved walking tolerance for patients with migraine symptoms, although the study specifically focused on arteritis.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can help alleviate migraine symptoms, but possible side effects include dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals. To minimize potential side effects, it's recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming the tea before or after surgery, as it may affect blood clotting and anesthesia.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba tea 2-3 times a day for migraines
Start with 1/4 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba per cup of tea
Gradually increase dosage to 1/2 teaspoon per cup as needed
Use Ginkgo biloba tea for 5-7 days to see migraine relief
Consult a doctor before using Ginkgo biloba tea regularly

preparations.herb_desc: Achillea millefolium teas, also known as yarrow tea, have been traditionally used to treat migraine headaches due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and volatile oils, help to relax blood vessels and reduce pain, thereby alleviating migraine symptoms. The benefits of this herbal preparation in treating migraine include its ability to reduce frequency and severity of attacks, improve sleep quality, and alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with migraines. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, Achillea millefolium teas offer a natural and effective treatment option for individuals suffering from migraine headaches.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Achillea millefolium flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried flowers and add to a teapot or heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the flowers and let steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the flowers.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, as needed for migraine relief.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Achillea millefolium teas can be used to treat migraines, but some possible side effects include stomach upset, dizziness, and allergic reactions in rare cases. When using Achillea millefolium teas to treat migraines, precautions to take include not consuming it in large quantities, avoiding it if you have sensitive stomach issues, and being aware of any allergic reactions that may occur.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Achillea millefolium tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1/4 teaspoon of dried herb per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase to 1/2 teaspoon per cup as needed.
Use for 7-10 days to manage frequent migraines.
Consult a healthcare professional for ongoing treatment.

preparations.herb_desc: Passiflora incarnata teas have been traditionally used to treat migraines due to their sedative and anti-anxiety properties, which help to alleviate the symptoms of this debilitating condition. The herbal preparation works by reducing inflammation and modulating the neurotransmitters that are involved in the pathophysiology of migraines, thereby providing relief from the pain and discomfort associated with this ailment. The bioactive constituents of Passiflora incarnata teas, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, have been shown to possess analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and anticonvulsant properties, which contribute to their effectiveness in treating migraines. Regular consumption of Passiflora incarnata teas has been found to provide numerous benefits in treating migraines, including rapid pain relief, reduced frequency and severity of attacks, and improved overall quality of life.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Passiflora incarnata flowers and 1 cup of water.
Wash the flowers with clean water to remove dirt.
Combine the flowers and water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and let simmer for 10-15 minutes, then strain the mixture.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day as needed for migraine relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Passiflora incarnata teas may have potential benefits in managing migraine due to the predicted pleiotropic effects of its phytoconstituents, including anticonvulsant, antidepressant, and nootropic activities.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Passiflora incarnata teas can be used to treat migraine, but its use may be associated with potential side effects such as drowsiness, dizziness, and stomach upset in some individuals. When using Passiflora incarnata teas to treat migraine, take precautions to avoid interactions with other medications, consume it in moderation, and be aware of its effects on cognitive function and driving ability.
preparation_usage: Drink 1-2 cups of Passiflora incarnata tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1/4 teaspoon of dried herb per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase the dose to 1/2 teaspoon per cup if needed.
Consume the tea for 2-4 weeks before re-evaluating migraine symptoms.
Consult a healthcare professional before making Passiflora incarnata tea a regular treatment.

preparations.herb_desc: Melissa officinalis teas, also known as lemon balm tea, have been used for centuries to treat migraine ailments due to their calming and soothing properties. The tea's ability to reduce stress and anxiety helps to alleviate migraine symptoms by promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension. Bioactive constituents such as rosmarinic acid, apigenin, and luteolin in Melissa officinalis teas have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with migraines. The benefits of using Melissa officinalis teas to treat migraines include improved sleep quality, reduced frequency and severity of migraine attacks, and a decrease in overall stress levels.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Melissa officinalis leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Add honey to taste, if needed, to sweeten the tea.
Drink the tea when you feel a migraine coming on or as directed by a healthcare professional.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melissa officinalis teas can be used to treat migraines, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals due to its high concentration of volatile oils. Precautions include taking the teas in moderation, avoiding consumption before driving or operating heavy machinery, and being aware of potential allergic reactions, especially when consumed in large quantities.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Melissa officinalis tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried herbs per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes before straining and drinking.
Continue usage for 7-10 days to manage migraine symptoms.
Consult a healthcare professional for dosages above 3 cups daily.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinnamomum verum teas, also known as Ceylon cinnamon, have been traditionally used to treat migraine ailments due to their analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cinnamomum verum teas, including cinnamaldehyde, eugenol, and linalool, help to treat migraines by reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and modulating neurotransmitter activity. The benefits of using Cinnamomum verum teas to treat migraines include reduced frequency and severity of attacks, improved sleep quality, and enhanced overall well-being. By incorporating Cinnamomum verum teas into one's daily routine, individuals may experience relief from migraine symptoms and improved quality of life.

Steep the powder in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup to remove the powder.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea and mix well.
Drink the tea immediately and repeat as needed, but not more than 2 cups per day.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinnamomum verum teas can potentially alleviate migraine symptoms, but its use may also lead to side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions should be taken when consuming Cinnamomum verum teas for migraines, including monitoring blood sugar levels due to its potential to lower blood sugar, and avoiding excessive consumption, as it may cause liver damage or interact with certain medications.

Start with 1 teaspoon of cinnamon powder per cup of tea.
Increase to 1.5 teaspoons if needed, but not exceeding 2 teaspoons.
Use 1-2 cups of tea per day for migraine relief, as needed.
Consult a doctor before using Cinnamomum verum tea for frequent migraines.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas, made from the fragrant herb rosemary, have been traditionally used to treat migraine ailments due to their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of rosemary tea, including carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, help to reduce inflammation and alleviate migraine symptoms by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and promoting relaxation. Drinking rosemary tea may also help to treat migraines by reducing muscle tension, improving blood flow, and modulating neurotransmitter activity. The benefits of using rosemary tea to treat migraines include reduced frequency and severity of episodes, improved overall well-being, and a natural, non-pharmacological approach to managing this debilitating condition.

Measure 1 teaspoon of honey (optional) and set aside.
Steep the Rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the leaves.
Add the honey (if using) and stir well to make the tea ready for consumption.

To minimize these side effects, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming the tea on an empty stomach to prevent stomach upset.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Rosmarinus officinalis tea for migraine treatment.

Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink 2-3 times a day.

preparations.herb_desc: Mentha x piperita teas, also known as peppermint teas, have been widely used to treat the migraine ailment due to their analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and vasodilatory properties. The active constituents of peppermint, including menthol and menthone, help to alleviate migraine symptoms by relaxing tense muscles, reducing pain, and improving blood flow to the brain. The bioactive compounds present in peppermint teas, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, also contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to reduce the severity and frequency of migraine attacks. Regular consumption of peppermint teas has been associated with several benefits, including reduced migraine frequency, improved sleep quality, and a decrease in overall stress levels.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of fresh Mentha x piperita leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried Mentha x piperita leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Pour the boiling water over the Mentha x piperita leaves in a cup.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the leaves from the tea.
Drink the tea when it's still warm, ideally 2-3 times a day to help with migraine relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Phytotherapy research : PTR", Mentha x piperita teas, also known as peppermint oil, showed positive, preliminary findings as an acute treatment for migraine.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Mentha x piperita teas can provide relief from migraine symptoms, but possible side effects include stomach upset, dizziness, and allergic reactions such as skin rashes or itching. When using Mentha x piperita teas to treat migraines, precautions should be taken, such as drinking the tea in moderation, avoiding it during pregnancy or breastfeeding, and being aware of potential interactions with other medications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Mentha x piperita tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1/4 teaspoon of dried herb per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase dosage to 1/2 teaspoon if needed and desired.
Consult a healthcare professional for proper dosage and advice.
Continue using for 2-4 weeks to observe migraine relief.

preparations.herb_desc: Matricaria chamomilla teas have been traditionally used to treat migraine due to their anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and calming properties. The soothing effect of these teas helps to alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing muscle tension and promoting relaxation, thereby providing relief from headaches and discomfort. The bioactive constituents of Matricaria chamomilla, including apigenin, luteolin, and bisabolol, are responsible for its therapeutic effects, which include reducing inflammation and modulating neurotransmitter activity to alleviate migraine pain. Regular consumption of Matricaria chamomilla teas may provide benefits in reducing the frequency and severity of migraines, making it a valuable natural remedy for individuals seeking alternative treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Matricaria chamomilla flowers.
Measure 1 tablespoon of the flowers and place them in a tea infuser.
Boil 1 cup of water in a kettle or on the stove.
Pour the boiling water over the tea infuser and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Matricaria chamomilla teas can be used to treat migraines, but some people may experience side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and dizziness due to its potential allergic reactions or interactions with other medications. preparations.herb_desc: Camellia sinensis teas, derived from the leaves of the Camellia sinensis plant, have been traditionally used to treat migraine ailments due to their analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and vasodilatory properties. The bioactive constituents present in these teas, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and catechins, help to alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the brain. The anti-inflammatory properties of Camellia sinensis teas, particularly the high levels of quercetin and catechins, help to reduce the severity of migraine attacks by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Regular consumption of Camellia sinensis teas has been shown to provide benefits in treating migraine, including reduced frequency and severity of attacks, improved sleep quality, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Camellia sinensis leaves, 1 cup of water, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Heat the water to 212°F (100°C) in a kettle or pot.
Place the Camellia sinensis leaves in the tea infuser or strainer.
Pour the hot water over the leaves and let it steep for 3-5 minutes, then remove the leaves.
Strain the tea into a cup and drink 1/2 cup every 2-3 hours to help manage migraine symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of biological regulators and homeostatic agents, Camellia sinensis teas may be beneficial for migraine due to its high antioxidant capacity, specifically the 50% methanolic extract which showed a significant radical scavenging DPPH assay with 78.95±7.12% potency.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Camellia sinensis teas can be a potentially effective remedy for alleviating migraine symptoms, but possible side effects may include dizziness, stomach upset, and nausea, particularly when consumed in large quantities or in combination with other medications. To minimize these side effects, it is recommended to start with a low dose, gradually increase the amount as needed, and avoid consuming the tea on an empty stomach, as well as to be cautious when combining it with other stimulants or medications that may exacerbate migraine symptoms.

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas, derived from the rhizome of the turmeric plant, have been traditionally used to treat migraine ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help to reduce the severity and frequency of migraines by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and scavenging free radicals that contribute to the development of migraine symptoms. The bioactive constituents of Curcuma longa teas, including curcuminoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, are responsible for their therapeutic effects, which help to reduce pain, inflammation, and oxidative stress associated with migraines. The benefits of using Curcuma longa teas to treat migraines include reduced pain intensity, improved quality of life, and a decrease in the reliance on conventional medications, making it a promising natural remedy for this debilitating condition.

Add the Turmeric powder to the boiling water and stir well.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and add honey to taste.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day as needed for migraine relief.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can be beneficial in treating migraines due to its anti-inflammatory properties, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea. The bioactive constituents present in this herbal preparation, including cinnamaldehyde, cinnamic acid, and eugenol, help to reduce migraine symptoms by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and blocking pain pathways. The consumption of Cinnamomum zeylanicum teas has been found to provide relief from migraine headaches by reducing inflammation, improving blood circulation, and modulating the body's pain response. The benefits of using Cinnamomum zeylanicum teas to treat migraines include reduced frequency and severity of attacks, improved overall well-being, and a natural alternative to conventional medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of Cinnamomum zeylanicum powder and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the powder in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to taste and mix well.
Drink the tea warm or at room temperature to help alleviate migraine symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Cinnamomum zeylanicum teas may have an anti-nociceptive effect, as it possesses an anti-nociceptive effect against both acetic acid-induced writhing and hot plate-induced thermal stimulation.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinnamomum zeylanicum teas can be used as a potential treatment for migraines, but they may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. When using Cinnamomum zeylanicum teas to treat migraines, it is essential to take precautions, such as starting with small amounts and gradually increasing the dose, avoiding consumption if you have a sensitive stomach, and being cautious when combined with other medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Hypericum perforatum teas, also known as St. John's Wort tea, have been traditionally used to treat migraines due to their anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. The flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids present in this herbal preparation help to reduce the frequency and severity of migraine attacks by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and promoting relaxation. The bioactive constituents, including hyperforin and hypericin, contribute to the tea's analgesic and anti-spasmodic effects, which help to alleviate migraine symptoms such as headache, nausea, and sensitivity to light. The benefits of using Hypericum perforatum teas to treat migraines include reduced pain, improved mood, and a decrease in the reliance on pharmaceutical medications, making it a popular natural remedy for migraine sufferers.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Hypericum perforatum leaves or 2 tablespoons of dried leaves.
Choose a clean glass jar and fill it with the gathered leaves.
Pour 2 cups of boiling water over the leaves in the jar.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into another container.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day for migraine relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Hypericum perforatum teas for migraine show relevant physiological activity but lack clinical studies for this purpose, and it's also noted that it's a hepatic enzyme inducer.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hypericum perforatum teas can cause several side effects when used to treat migraines, including stomach upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with blood thinners, which may exacerbate bleeding. To minimize potential risks, take Hypericum perforatum teas in moderation, avoid combining it with other medications, and monitor your body's response to ensure that it does not worsen your condition.

preparations.herb_desc: Citrus reticulata teas, derived from the peel of the mandarin orange, have been traditionally used to treat migraine symptoms due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The herbal preparation helps to alleviate migraine pain by reducing inflammation and modulating neurotransmitter activity, thus providing relief from severe headache and other associated symptoms. Citrus reticulata teas contain bioactive constituents such as limonoids and flavonoids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that contribute to their therapeutic effects. The benefits of using Citrus reticulata teas to treat migraines include reduced frequency and severity of episodes, improved quality of life, and a natural, non-pharmacological approach to managing this debilitating condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of dried Citrus reticulata peel, 1 cup of boiling water, and honey (optional).
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried Citrus reticulata peel in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the peel. Add honey to taste, if needed.
Let the tea cool down. You can refrigerate it for later use.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, as needed to help manage migraine symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Phytotherapy research : PTR, Citrus reticulata teas, specifically citron, presented positive, preliminary findings as a prophylactic treatment for migraine.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Citrus reticulata teas can be a potential treatment for migraine relief, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to its high concentration of citric acid and other active compounds. To use Citrus reticulata teas safely, it is recommended to start with a small dose, avoid taking it on an empty stomach, and be cautious when consuming it with other medications or substances that may interact with its active ingredients.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat migraine ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with migraines. The herbal preparation's ability to modulate the immune system and reduce oxidative stress also contributes to its effectiveness in treating this condition. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea purpurea, including alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and volatile oils, are responsible for its therapeutic effects, which help to alleviate migraine symptoms such as pain, sensitivity, and nausea. Regular consumption of Echinacea purpurea teas has been shown to provide relief from migraine episodes and promote overall well-being, making it a popular natural remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Echinacea purpurea flowers.
Use 1 cup of boiling water to make the tea.
Pour the boiling water over the Echinacea flowers in a cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 3 times a day to help with migraine relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Molecular nutrition & food research, Echinacea purpurea teas for migraine do not appear to be a risk to consumers, particularly as the low potential to generate cytochrome P450 drug-herb interactions has been observed.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to treat migraines, but possible side effects may include nausea, dizziness, and allergic reactions, such as skin rashes or itching. To use this herbal preparation safely, take precautions to monitor your body's response, avoid consuming it during pregnancy or breastfeeding, and be cautious when combining it with other medications, including blood thinners and sedatives.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas have been used for centuries to treat migraine ailments, and its properties as a natural anti-inflammatory and vasoconstrictor help to alleviate symptoms. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as quinine, which helps to constrict blood vessels and reduce inflammation, thereby relieving migraine pain. By reducing the frequency and severity of migraine episodes, Cinchona officinalis teas can also help to improve overall quality of life and reduce the reliance on pharmaceutical medications. The benefits of using Cinchona officinalis teas to treat migraines include a reduction in symptoms, improved sleep quality, and a natural approach to managing this debilitating condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Cinchona officinalis bark and a cup of boiling water.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water and pour it over the dried Cinchona officinalis bark in a cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth.
Discard the dried Cinchona officinalis bark and pour the tea into a cup.
Drink the tea as needed, ideally 2-3 times a day, to help manage migraine symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to American family physician, Cinchona officinalis teas for migraine have not been proven effective or studied rigorously, suggesting that more research is needed to understand their efficacy.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinchona officinalis teas can be an effective remedy for migraine relief, but its use may be associated with side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to the high concentration of quinine. To minimize potential side effects, individuals should start with small doses, monitor their body's response, and avoid consuming the tea too frequently, especially if they have pre-existing kidney or liver conditions.

intro_study: According to Phytotherapy research : PTR, teas for migraine, such as those containing feverfew, butterbur, curcumin, menthol/peppermint oil, coriander, citron, and chamomile, may present potential options to enhance the treatment of migraine, although further high-quality research is necessary to confirm their efficacy and safety.
